other_observers: Brenda Linfield; I knew of a pattern of sightings of this species in the town of Rocky Ford, based on write-ups in the CFO quarterly journal.
specific_location: in the town of Rocky Ford
description: It looked something like a miniature Mourning Dove, with a very similar color to that species.  Differences between this bird and a Mourning Dove:  The flanks and the sides of the breast were barred.  It had very scaly underparts. Because the bird was above me, I did not get a good look at its upperparts.  It had a long, square-tipped tail.  When the bird was perched, I could only see a single whitish outer tail feather (on each side).  It had a very distinctive, two-note call.Behaviors: perched in the foliage of a conifer in a neighborhoodCall: distinctive two-note callPlumage: adult
